evals.prompt: Create a short presentation about Q1 2026 Logistics performance. Include a cover slide, an executive summary with 3 key takeaways (on-time delivery improved by 4%, courier satisfaction up 12 points, average delivery time down to 28 minutes), a data slide showing quarterly metrics, and a closing slide. My name is Sarah Chen and I'm a Senior PM.
evals.expected_output: A Google Slides presentation cloned from the JET template with approximately 5-7 slides: Logistics cover page, executive summary, data/metrics slide, and a closing slide on JET Orange. All placeholder text replaced with actual content. Returns a shareable link.
expectations: The presentation is created by cloning template 1M-8jgsBKitaL6pkVg_78DcoFm__9_MoEmmkpazKkv9k
The Logistics cover page (slide index 3, objectId g329ad47a42a_0_362) is kept; other cover pages are deleted
Template-internal slides (13, 28-34, 157-160, 161-175) are deleted
The executive summary slide (index 40) is used for key takeaways
A data or graph slide is used for quarterly metrics
The presentation starts with a cover slide and ends with a closing/thanks slide
Placeholder text like 'Month Year', 'Name Surname', 'Lorem ipsum' is replaced with actual content
A shareable Google Slides URL is returned to the user
The content uses active voice and JET brand tone (no banned words like 'cheap', 'fast food')
All text uses sentence case (not Title Case) — only first word and proper nouns capitalised
The author name 'Sarah Chen' appears on the cover slide

evals.prompt: Make me a research readout deck. The project was 'Partner Onboarding UX Audit' for the Partner UX team. We interviewed 15 restaurant partners across 4 markets (UK, NL, DE, ES). Key findings: 1) Partners find the menu upload process confusing (8/15 needed support calls), 2) Verification takes too long — average 5 days vs promised 48 hours, 3) Partners love the real-time order dashboard. Include some partner quotes. I'm Alex Rivera, UX Researcher.
evals.expected_output: A research readout presentation with 10-15 slides: Partner UX cover, contents page, project context with methodology, executive summary, findings sections with dividers, quote slides with partner quotes, highs and lows, recommendations, and closing slide. All on-brand.
expectations: The presentation is created by cloning the JET template
The Partner UX cover page (slide index 1, objectId g31669fcce71_0_0) is kept
A project context slide (37, 38, or 39) is used to describe the methodology
An executive summary slide (40) is used for key takeaways
Quote slides (43 or 44) are used for partner quotes
The Highs (56) and/or The Lows (55) slides are used for findings
Three-point or insights slides (50, 51, or 52) are used for key findings
The deck follows a logical structure with section dividers between major sections
Divider slides separate major sections
The content avoids market-specific colour issues (Turmeric avoided for Spain)
All text uses sentence case (not Title Case) — only first word and proper nouns capitalised
Template-internal slides are deleted from the final presentation

evals.prompt: I need a quick 5-slide deck about a new feature we're launching: 'Smart Reorder' for the Customer UX team. It lets customers reorder their last meal with one tap. Just need a cover, what it is, why it matters, timeline, and a thank you slide. Keep it punchy. I'm Jordan Lee, Product Designer.
evals.expected_output: A concise 5-slide presentation: Customer UX cover, feature description slide, value proposition slide, timeline/roadmap, and closing. Short and punchy copy in JET brand voice.
expectations: The presentation is created by cloning the JET template
The Customer UX cover page (slide index 0, objectId g329ad47a42a_0_0) is kept
The presentation has approximately 5 slides (not bloated with unnecessary slides)
A timeline or roadmap slide (47, 68, 151, or 152) is included
A closing/thanks slide (85 or 176) is included
The presentation follows a logical structure with appropriate slide types
Copy is concise and punchy as requested
All text uses sentence case (not Title Case) — only first word and proper nouns capitalised
All template-internal slides are deleted
A shareable URL is returned
